data.title: Wall Street rises as key labor report comes largely in line
data.description:
The stock market inched higher on Friday as the key jobs report came largely as expected. 

S&P 500 (SP500) +0.2%, the Dow (DJI) +0.2%, and the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) +0.1%.

December nonfarm payrolls growth came in roughly in line, while the unemployment rate dipped to 4.4%.

Treasury yields advanced across the curve. The U.S. 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) rose to 3.51%, up 1.7 basis points. The benchmark 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) climbed to 4.19%, adding 1.3 basis points. The 30-year Treasury yield (US30Y) ticked higher to 4.86%, up 1.6 basis points.

data.title: Metals Creek acquires 0.5% NSR on Tillex property from Blue Moon Metals
data.description:
• Metals Creek (MCREF) on Friday announced that it acquired a 0.5% net smelter return royalty on the Tillex property in Ontario from Blue Moon Metals.
  • The company had entered into a purchase agreement with Savant in November 2008 to acquire an 85% interest in the Tillex property, which the company had the right to purchase.
  • The company has now exercised that right, acquiring the 0.5% NSR in exchange for issuing 50,000 Metals Creek common shares to Blue Moon Metals.
  • Shares -26.33%.

data.title: Real estate tech stocks gap up amid Trump's housing relief push
data.description:
A number of housing-sensitive fintech stocks jumped in Friday premarket trading on the back of U.S. President Donald Trump's latest effort to bring down housing costs.

Offerpad Solutions (OPAD) shares surged 55% to $2.35 apiece, loanDepot (LDI) spiked 17%, Opendoor Technologies (OPEN) +11%, Blend Labs (BLND) +6% and LendingTree (TREE) +5.9% at press time.

The swings come after Trump on Thursday said he's instructing unnamed representatives to acquire $200B in mortgage bonds. His move is aimed at narrowing mortgage spreads and lowering borrowing costs, which could be a boon for loan origination volume and transaction demand for the aforementioned companies.

data.title: Bitmine Immersion Technologies appoints Young Kim as CFO, COO
data.description:
• Bitmine Immersion Technologies (BMNR) appointed Young Kim as chief financial officer, chief operating officer, and a member of its board of directors, effective immediately.
  • Kim brings over 20 years of investment experience, including senior portfolio management roles at Axiom Investors (2021–2025) and Columbia Threadneedle Investments (2011–2021).
  • Bitmine now owns 3.43% of the ETH token supply, two-thirds of the way to the 'Alchemy of 5%'.

data.title: Gorilla Technology updates share repurchase program, $9.6M capacity remaining
data.description:
• Gorilla Technology Group (GRRR) has repurchased ~$5M of shares, or about 354,000 shares, since increasing its buyback program to $20M in Q4 2025.
  • Total repurchases under the program now stand at ~$10.4M, leaving around $9.6M in remaining authorization.
  • Gorilla noted reduced share liquidity, attributing it to long-term shareholders holding shares.
  • The company will temporarily pause repurchases until after releasing its Full Year 2025 financial statements, with plans to resume if shares remain undervalued.

data.title: The worst may be behind Airbnb, but Booking still the better value -- analysts
data.description:
The worst is likely behind Airbnb (ABNB), and after lagging peers like Expedia Group (EXPE) and Booking Holdings (BKNG) the last two years, the stock should enter a period of conservative gains thanks to its “Reserve Now, Pay Later” option and availability during the 2026 North America FIFA World Cup.

“We see diminished downside risks to shares and a few potential upside drivers to room night growth that could position Airbnb to deliver best-in-class room night growth among the scaled online travel peer set,” Barclays’ Trevor Young said in his note upgrading the stock to Equal Weight from Underweight.

Wells Fargo’s Ken Gawrelski agrees that there is limited downside left in Airbnb (ABNB), but the timing to capitalize on the opportunities for growth in the alternative accommodations marketplace is uncertain, preventing Wells Fargo from becoming more constructive on the stock.

Accordingly, Gawrelski also upgrades Airbnb (ABNB) to Equal Weight from Underweight.

Despite the more upbeat – albeit cautious -- outlook for Airbnb rivals Expedia (EXPE) and Booking (BKNG) continue to expand their “alternative accommodation” offerings, nipping at the heels of the current leader in this category. Of the three, Barcalys’ Young favors Booking (BKNG).

“If one is constructive on alternative accommodations, why not buy the #2 player (Booking) that is growing faster and trades at a discount,” Young says.

Airbnb (ABNB) is still a monoline business, Young adds, and hasn’t proven its ability to scale in adjacencies, including its few iterations of its experiences initiative.

Along with an upgrade, Wells Fargo and Barclays both lift their target price for Airbnb (ABNB) by 8.5% and 12% to $128 and $120, respectively.

Shares are expected to open modestly higher on Friday with Expedia (EXPE) outperforming and poised to set another all-time high.

data.title: Apple CEO's compensation drops slightly in 2025; board recommends shareholders vote no on China audit
data.description:
Apple (AAPL) CEO Tim Cook's full compensation package for 2025 showed a slight decline in 2025 compared to 2024, while other key executives' compensations also demonstrated a slim drop.

Cook's compensation totaled $74.3M in 2025, according to a filing with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission. It totaled $74.6M in 2024. Cook's base salary of $3M has remained the same since 2016.

"Mr. Cook's outstanding equity awards include a retirement vesting provision approved by the People and Compensation Committee in recognition of the unique impact of his leadership decisions on the long-term growth and success of Apple and to facilitate thoughtful succession planning when appropriate," the filing said. "Retirement is defined as a termination of employment after reaching at least 60 years of age and completing at least 10 years of service with Apple. Mr. Cook has met these requirements."

Other top Apple executives had very slight declines in their compensation. Kate Adams, senior vice president and general counsel and secretary, package was worth $27.03M compared to $27.18M in 2024. Deirdre O'Brien, senior vice president of Retail and People, received $27.05M compared to $27.18M in 2024. Kevan Parekh, who is in his first year as chief financial officer, received $22.46M. Former chief financial officer Luca Maestri received $15.48M.

Apple plans to hold its 2026 annual shareholders meeting at 8 a.m. PT on February 24.

Shareholders are expected to vote on five items of business in the upcoming annual meeting. The board recommends voting for the first four items, which include election of directors, appointment of an independent registered public accounting firm, executive compensation and Apple's non-employee director stock plan.

However, the board recommends shareholders vote against a proposed "China entanglement audit."

"With escalating U.S.–China trade tensions, Apple could face steep tariff increases," according to the shareholder proposal. "Analysts warn tariffs on Chinese imports could hit as high as 145%, severely inflating production costs. One article projects this could add $10B annually to iPhone expenses. While Apple is apparently shifting some production to India and Vietnam, these regions still depend heavily on Chinese parts, and manufacturing costs are 5–10% higher."

The proposal also warns that China may restrict support from Chinese engineers and suppliers. It points out that nearly 20% of Apple's revenue stems from Mainland China, Hong Kong and Taiwan, and a regional conflict could disrupt that. It also mentions supply chain disruptions, China's control over rare earth minerals and Chinese espionage.

"Shareholders request that the Board of Directors of Apple conduct an evaluation and issue a report within the next year, at reasonable cost and excluding confidential information, assessing risks and costs associated with the company’s continued entanglement with the People’s Republic of China," the filing said.

But the board maintains that Apple already provides extensive information on its international operations.

"The proposal is highly prescriptive and attempts to inappropriately restrict Apple's ability to manage its own ordinary business operations and business strategies," the board said.

data.title: Eli Lilly in pact with Schrödinger, Revvity for AI-driven drug discovery
data.description:
Schrödinger (SDGR) and Revvity (RVTY) on Friday announced collaborations with Eli Lilly (LLY) to integrate their respective drug development software offerings with the Indiana-based pharma giant’s TuneLab AI-based drug discovery platform.

According to Schrödinger (SDGR), its enterprise informatics platform, LiveDesign, will become a priority interface for biotech firms accessing LLY’s TuneLab, which leverages AI capabilities to accelerate drug discovery.

The New York-based company said that while a third party will host TuneLab on its platform, LLY and its partner firms can keep their proprietary data secure thanks to a privacy-focused method called federated learning.

“We are pleased to be partnering with Lilly to expand the use of digital drug design methods and ultimately drive greater impact for patients,” added Karen Akinsanya, SDGR’s president of R&D, therapeutics, and chief strategy officer, partnerships.

Meanwhile, Revvity (RVTY) said that Lilly TuneLab will be made available through its Revvity Signals platform, allowing the biotech community to benefit from Lilly’s advanced predictive models.

Additionally, the companies will jointly provide funding to selected biotech participants, allowing them to access Schrödinger’s (SDGR) Signals One, Signals Xynthetica, and other software offerings, as well as modeling credits.

Update 9:15 AM ET: Adds Lilly- Revvity partnership

data.title: TerraVest acquires KBK Industries for $90M
data.description:
• TerraVest Industries (TVK:CA) acquired KBK Industries, a Texas-based manufacturer of fiberglass and steel storage tanks, for US$90M in cash, subject to a post-closing working capital adjustment.

  • The purchase price represents ~5.6x KBK’s trailing twelve-month EBITDA.

  • KBK operates manufacturing facilities in Kansas and serves C-Store, Agricultural, Chemical, Infrastructure, and Energy markets across the U.S.

  • The acquisition complements TerraVest’s existing fiberglass tank operations in Canada and the U.S., including its Composite USA business acquired in January 2025.

  • TerraVest expects to enhance KBK’s sales channels by leveraging its established relationships in the C-Store market.

  • The deal adds geographic diversification and helps mitigate tariff exposure across TerraVest’s platform.

  • TerraVest funded the acquisition using its existing credit facility.

data.title: Semiconductors in focus as Mizuho downgrades Qualcomm, changes price targets
data.description:
Semiconductor stocks were in focus on Friday as Mizuho downgraded Qualcomm (QCOM) and tweaked its price targets on a host of other companies, including Micron (MU) and Western Digital (WDC). 

The firm cut its rating on Qualcomm to Neutral from Outperform and cut its price target to $175 from $200, noting it has “handset headwinds,” as it is expected to lose share in Apple (AAPL) devices this year and next. Qualcomm shares fell 1.4% in premarket trading.

Mizuho is positive on most of the rest of the sector, at least in the first-half of the year, as the artificial intelligence spending trade is set to continue. “We see potential for a strong 1H26E as AI spend, [wafer fab equipment] and memory cycles all expected to remain strong, powering tier1/2 CSP, enterprise/sovereign AI server ramps,” Mizuho analysts wrote in a note to clients. “Into 2H26E, we see potential challenges with 1) higher volatility around new product launches/ramps with Rubin/ MI400, 2) US midterm elections (11/3/2026), and 3) slower Fed rate cuts.”

The firm's top picks for 2026 are Nvidia (NVDA), Lumentum (LITE) and Broadcom (AVGO), and it also likes Microchip (MCHP) and Lam Research (LRCX) as “key outperformers.”

In addition, Mizuho also changed its price targets on several stocks. They are as follows:

  • Applied Materials (AMAT) was upped to $275 from $245.
  • Lam Research was raised to $220 from $200.
  • Lumentum was raised to $455 from $325.
  • MKS Inc. (MKSI) was raised to $215 from $195.
  • Micron (MU) was raised to $390 from $290.
  • Nvidia was raised to $275 from $245.
  • Onsemi (ON) was raised to $72 from $62.
  • Sandisk (SNDK) was raised to $410 from $250.
  • Seagate (STX) was raised to $370 from $290.
  • Super Micron (SMCI) was lowered to $31 from $45.
  • Western Digital (WDC) was raised to $240 from $180.
  • Qorvo (QRVO) was cut to $85 from $93. 
  • Skyworks Solutions (SWKS) was cut to $65 from $73.
